text: Fill in the blanks about radiodiagnosis principles and imaging (AN54.1-4).
questions: The five radiographic densities from most to least opaque are: *metal/bone*, soft tissue, *fat/adipose*, *air/gas*, and foreign body.
Free gas under the diaphragm on an erect abdominal X-ray indicates *perforation/perforated viscus* and constitutes a *surgical/surgical emergency* emergency requiring immediate intervention.

*Barium sulfate/barium* is used to coat the GI mucosa for contrast studies but is contraindicated when *perforation/bowel perforation* is suspected, in which case *water-soluble iodine/gastrografin* contrast is used instead.
CT uses *Hounsfield/HU* units to quantify tissue density, where bone measures approximately +400 to *+1000/1000* HU and air measures *-1000/minus 1000* HU.
